fr.elements: Main Building; Landscaped Area; Detached Structure; Open Green Space; Driveway
fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency response effectiveness on the property could be facilitated by addressing the dense vegetation that may obstruct access and visibility for emergency responders. Regular trimming and maintenance around the driveway and other key access routes could ensure clear passage and improve sight lines. Establishing additional access points where feasible may enhance accessibility and provide multiple routes for responder movement. Ensuring the landscaped area remains free from obstructions could assist in navigation and staging, allowing responders to reach the main building and detached structures more efficiently. Additionally, maintaining open green spaces with minimal obstruction can support effective staging areas for emergency operations. These actions could collectively improve responder access, mobility, and operational coordination during emergency situations.

wildfire.conditions: Tree Cluster; Vegetated Perimeter; Scattered Trees; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Open Terrain; Vegetation Adjacent to Fence; Open Fuel Break; Vegetated Drainage; Dense Tree Canopy; Surrounding Open Space
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space around the property could involve managing the tree cluster and dense tree canopy to reduce fuel continuity and increase vertical and horizontal separations between treetops. Vegetation adjacent to the structure and fence should be strategically managed to minimize potential fire exposure and support structural protection. The open fuel break should be maintained to provide an effective barrier against fire spread through the property. Regularly clearing vegetated drainage areas can reduce the accumulation of combustible material that might otherwise channel fire towards the property. The open terrain and surrounding open space are valuable in promoting defensible space and could be preserved to help interrupt fire continuity. Continuously monitoring and managing scattered trees throughout the property would also support suppression operations and overall wildfire resilience.
